metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found some amazing activities for your little one, from exploring underwater adventures at the Horniman Museum to enjoying a lively barn dance in Camden. Plus, there are plenty of free museum activities and outdoor spaces to explore, making it a budget-friendly and exciting weekend!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed environment for toddlers to explore books and stories at their own pace.

transport.fromHackney: Buses and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before you go!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed for children, including spaces for imagination, play, and design. Explore childhood objects and take part in activities such as storytelling and crafting.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits, a Mini Museum for under 2s, and various activities make it perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground.  Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play areas and storytelling sessions that can spark your child's creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Stratford
transport.tip: Check the schedule for Baby Storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small and beautifully designed playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers with its gentle and nature-inspired design.

transport.fromHackney: Buses or Central Line to Mile End
transport.tip: Combine with a walk in Mile End Park.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A hidden gem near Brick Lane with a community feel, featuring a variety of farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love animals, and this farm offers a chance to see goats, pigs, chickens, and more in an accessible location.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Brick Lane or Overground to Shoreditch High Street
transport.tip: Check out their line up of Easter activities closer to the time.

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre: Last Unicorn Airways
activities.description: An immersive installation and theatre show.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers an immersive and imaginative experience perfect for engaging young children.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Limehouse
transport.tip: Check show times and book in advance.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Listen, play and make some noise in this informal session.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for babies and toddlers to explore sounds and music in a relaxed setting.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Arrive early as it's first-come, first-served.

activities.title: Planet Play at Chickenshed
activities.description: Interactive, sensory show.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for very young children with sensory elements to capture their attention.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line to Southgate
transport.tip: Check the Chickenshed website for ticket prices and availability.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore a vast collection of natural history specimens, including dinosaurs and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, and wide open spaces to roam.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Holborn, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book a free timed ticket in advance.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits covering science, technology, and medicine.  Visit the Garden and Pattern Pod for under 8's.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden and Pattern Pod, interactive exhibits, and iconic objects make it perfect for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Holborn, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit the Garden in the basement for toddler-friendly fun.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history displays, musical instruments, and a beautiful garden with farm animals. Don't miss the Aquarium and Butterfly House.
activities.whyItWorks: Gardens, animals, and interactive exhibits offer something for every toddler.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Check the calendar for special events and family activities.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, featuring an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a safe and fun environment for toddlers to explore and play.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Kings Cross or Piccadilly Line to Russell Square
transport.tip: Adults only allowed if accompanying a child.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore two million years of human history and culture.
activities.whyItWorks: Egyptian galleries with mummies and hieroglyphs are particularly captivating for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road
transport.tip: Pick up an activity backpack or Museum Explorer Trail from the Family Desk.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A riverside hub of arts, fun, and food with kids' concerts, festivals, creative workshops, hands-on installations, and space to scoot or picnic.
activities.whyItWorks: Welcoming, accessible, and packed with energy, with lots of free activities and open spaces.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Southbank or Tube to Waterloo
transport.tip: Check the events calendar before heading out.

activities.title: Tate Modern
activities.description: Explore modern and contemporary art with diverse collections and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The Turbine Hall and kids' room with interactive screens offer engaging experiences for children.

transport.fromHackney: Buses to Southbank or Tube to Blackfriars or Southwark
transport.tip: Check for family workshops and activities.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Visit a stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: The greenery and plants make it a sensory experience, and the views are impressive.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Monument or Tower Hill
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as they are required.

activities.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
activities.description: A new spin on Shakespeare's A Midsummer Night's Dream.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic tale reimagined for a younger audience at a dedicated children's theatre.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend performances.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and imaginative, allowing children to see their own toys come to life.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to London Bridge
transport.tip: Encourage your child to bring a favorite toy to participate.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An outdoor energy-burner, it motivates kids to walk and explore with the promise of chocolate.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Go early in the day – the grounds are calmer before lunch.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: A fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Allow 2–3 hours if you're doing it properly.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Cutty Sark
activities.price: Free with entry to the ship
activities.description: Take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's epic voyages, while you explore the decks and keep your eyes peeled for the hidden eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore a historic ship and solve a nautical code for a chocolatey treat.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Most suitable for young explorers age five+ and their families.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Pick up a trail card, and as you wander around, find the missing letters to crack a secret password. There are also Easter craft activities throughout the holidays.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun Easter egg hunt combined with a visit to see the animals at London Zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town
transport.tip: Check for Easter craft activities throughout the holidays.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: A Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: A unique experience combining afternoon tea with a Peppa Pig theme.

transport.fromHackney: Check the starting location when booking
transport.tip: Book in advance as this is a popular event.

activities.title: Fairy Tales
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Brings classic fairy tales to life through an interactive exhibition.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to King's Cross St. Pancras or Euston
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to secure your spot.
